Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Direct assay
Cholesterol in HDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma

tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows significant hyperglycemia with normal HbA1c, suggesting possible acute glucose elevation. Lipid profile is markedly abnormal (high total cholesterol, LDL, triglycerides, and low HDL), indicating increased cardiovascular risk. Mild hyperchloremia and elevated platelet distribution width are noted but of unclear clinical significance. Renal function, electrolytes, and hematology are otherwise within normal limits. Recommend clinical correlation for diabetes and dyslipidemia management.
Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Direct assay
